question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single GET /api/v1/contracts/results/{transactionIdOrHash}/opcodes request with Accept-Encoding: gzip and stack=true&memory=true&storage=true, causing opcodeService.processOpcodeCall() to trigger a full EVM re-execution that holds the single global opcodeRateLimitBucket token for the entire request duration (up to requestTimeout=10s), thereby blocking all other users from the endpoint for a period exceeding 500% of the average Hedera block time (~2-3s)?
